parityMeta.describes: iOS-17e-0 progression parity scaffold — plateau / stall history. Five recent completed push-a sessions log the same main compound at a FLAT top weight (60 kg every session) with neutral readiness and no training gap. The real TS trainingDecisionEngine reads these performed sets (isE1rmTrendUp=false: recent 3-session top average == older average, not greater) and HOLDS instead of progressing -> progressionMode='hold'. The direct contrast to progressive-overload-v1: identical session count + cadence, only the weight trend differs, so the golden isolates 'no upward trend => no add-weight'. DECODE-ONLY on the Swift side in 17e-0; compute-assert lands as 17e-1~5 port the progression cluster.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: Synthetic AppData. Five completed push-a sessions spaced 3 days (daysAgo 14/11/8/5/2), same exercise, flat top weight (60 kg) -> no e1rm trend (recent==older). Most recent 2 days before the clock (no gap -> normal phase). Neutral readiness. sessionIntent='normal-session', progressionMode='hold' (the stall signal).
